data.description:
## Context

Direct grep of the four reviewer delta prompts confirms that `pipefail` is mentioned in passing and `mktemp` appears only as a cleanup pattern. None of the prompts contain `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`, `hermetic`, `bash 3`, `declare -A`, `SIGPIPE`, `BASH_SOURCE`, runner-equivalence, or env-var guard items as named checklist entries. Reviewers cannot flag what their rubric does not name — every Pattern 1 / Pattern 4 ticket in the recent corpus was caught by CI or release gate, not by code review.

This epic adds named rubric items to the reviewer delta prompts so reviewer agents flag antipattern dimensions they currently miss. Pure additive change; pairs with **New Epic C** (override discipline) which constrains how findings are dismissed.

See `docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md`, `docs/process-gaps-4-26.md`, and `docs/new-epics-4-26.md` for full provenance.

## Cited evidence

**Verification of rubric coverage gap (G4):**
- `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-delta-deep-correctness.md` lines 109-112 — only `set -euo pipefail` mentioned; no env-var guard, bash version, BASH_SOURCE, SIGPIPE, runner items
- `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-delta-deep-hygiene.md` lines 49-62 — strict mode mentioned; no hermeticity, version compat, or runner items
- `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-delta-test-quality.md` lines 54-73 — bash test patterns reviewed; no cross-runner equivalence requirement
- `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-delta-deep-verification.md` lines 54-63 — assert helpers and traps mentioned; no hermeticity item, no platform-matrix item
- `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/behavioral-testing-standard.md` lines 67-77 — Rule 3 "execute and assert outcome"; no requirement for multiple runners or clean env
- `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-delta-standard.md:35` — `# isolation-ok:` exists but no rubric for runner changes

**Bug classes that reviewers consistently miss because no rubric item targets them:**
- All Pattern 1 tickets (`fe45-0b58`, `09d8-11f0`, `82ad-7bb3`, `97a7-4504`, `4191-9096`, `0a47-9631`, `a40e-ab52`)
- All Pattern 2 tickets (`7225-7708`, `bf39-4494`, `e2b6-1059`, `e8a9-136f`)
- All Pattern 4 tickets (`e241-41b2`, `999e-cf69`)
- All Pattern 7 tickets (`a190-d780`)

**Discovery channel breakdown** (from `docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md` cross-cutting observations):
- 16 of 22 recent closed bugs detected at release gate (`scripts/release.sh` precondition #8)
- Code review caught only 3 of 37
- The pre-commit gate stack and the reviewer rubrics jointly leak these regression classes

## Mitigations included

- **G1-M2** — Hermeticity items in `reviewer-delta-deep-correctness.md` and `reviewer-delta-deep-hygiene.md`: env-var guarding (each `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`/`$REPO_ROOT`/`$BASH_SOURCE`-derived path under `set -u` must be guarded with `:-` default or asserted at script entry); severity floor: correctness 2
- **G2-M3** — Cross-runner change item in `reviewer-delta-standard.md` and `reviewer-delta-deep-correctness.md`: when diff touches `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/`, `validate.sh`, or `run-hook-tests.sh`, confirm corresponding update to runner-equivalence corpus and paired update to every other runner sharing the affected semantic
- **G4-M1** — Platform-axis sub-section in `reviewer-delta-deep-correctness.md`: bash 3.2 incompatibilities (`declare -A`, `${var^^}`, `mapfile`/`readarray`, `[[ =~ ]]` capture groups, `coproc`), SIGPIPE-safety on pipefail, BASH_SOURCE resilience under symlink
- **G4-M2** — Cross-platform check in `reviewer-delta-deep-verification.md`: every test must be runnable in (a) bash 3.2, (b) clean env (`unset CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT HOME`), (c) under both bash-runner.sh and suite-engine.sh; tests depending on bash 4+ syntax must declare `# requires-bash4`
- **G11-M3** — Config doc paired-update item in `reviewer-delta-standard.md`: if diff adds/modifies a key in any `*.conf`, confirm matching entry in `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md`; missing or stale doc = `maintainability` finding at severity 3
- **G12-M2** — Pipe-to-grep under pipefail item in `reviewer-delta-deep-correctness.md`: for every `echo … | grep …` or `printf … | grep …` in diff, confirm script does not have `pipefail` set OR construct uses here-string (`<<<`)

## Proposed success criteria

1. Six named rubric items added to the reviewer delta prompts as listed above; each item names the bug class, cites the originating ticket(s), and specifies a severity floor
2. A reviewer-prompt regression test asserts each item's heading is present (parallel to skill-section manifest pattern in `a8e8-0b23`)
3. Findings emitted under the new rubric items are tagged with rubric source (e.g., `rubric:platform-axis`) so adoption can be measured via `review-stats.sh`
4. Documentation: `REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md` updated to enumerate the new rubric dimensions
5. Coverage: re-running review against the cited Pattern 1/2/4 tickets would have surfaced the same defects the original review missed; documented as regression scenarios

## Coordination notes

- Pure additive change to existing reviewer prompts — does not interact with **New Epic C** (override discipline) which constrains how reviewers *use* findings
- Uses the new rubric flag injection mechanism added to `ab57-b534` (G4-M3): once `ab57-b534` ships, implementation-plan can inject these flags at task-classification time
- The mechanical hooks in **New Epic A** are the static counterpart; B is the human-loop ring of defense

## References

- `docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md` Patterns 1, 2, 4, 7
- `docs/process-gaps-4-26.md` G4 (verified verbatim)
- `docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md` G1-M2, G2-M3, G4-M1, G4-M2, G11-M3, G12-M2
- `docs/new-epics-4-26.md` § New Epic B
data.title: Reviewer rubric expansion: platform, runner, hermeticity, config, pipefail dimensions
